title: Thai Green Curry
method: Cut chicken thighs into long thin slices. Mix with 1 tbsp curry paste and season with salt
Heat 1 tbsp oil in a pan, add chicken and fry for 5 mins until cooked through. Transfer cooked chicken to a plate
Add remaining oil to pan and fry onions for 10 mins until softened. Transfer to plate

Scoop top third (super creamy top part) of both cans of coconut milk into pan. Heat over medium low heat and slowly bring to a simmer until the water and fat separate. Add curry paste and stir fry for 3 mins
Add onions back into pan followed by the rest of the coconut milk and fish sauce. Stir the mixture and bring to a boil
Add chicken back into pan, bring to boil then reduce heat to a simmer
Add sugarsnap peas into curry, stir for 1 minute then remove pan from heat. Serve with rice
notes: Add bamboo shoots with the sugarsnap peas
Can use galangal instead of ginger

title: Korean Beef
method: Melt 1 tbsp butter in pan, add garlic and stir fry until fragrant
Add minced beef and cook until browned
In a separate cup mix together brown sugar, soy sauce, and gochujang
Add sauce and ginger to beef, and stir until coated. Serve with rice
notes: Serve with boiled broccoli - 2 broccoli for a week's worth

title: Pork Belly Sandwiches
method: Cut the skin off the pork belly and pat dry with kitchen roll
Rub belly with olive oil, then season with dry rub ingredients
Wrap pork belly with foil
Air fry for 50 minutes at 190C - Tighten foil at 30 minutes
Remove from air fryer, and allow to rest for 15 minutes
Make the sauce - mix mustard, honey, brown sugar and paprika into saucepan and bring to simmer, whisking until dissolved
Cube the pork and add to the sauce
notes: Pork can be baked in oven for 1 hour at 140C then 1.5 hours at 240C

title: Cream Corn Chicken
ingredients: ½ can of water (using the cream corn can)
method: Cut chicken thighs into small cubes.
Season the cubed chicken with salt and garlic powder. Leave for 15 minutes.
Fry the chicken until golden brown.
Decrease the heat and add in the 2 cans of cream corn plus half a can of water (using one of the corn cans to measure).
Increase heat until the mixture comes to a boil.
Stir in thickening granules (or your preferred thickener) until the sauce reaches the desired consistency.

title: Lasagne
ingredients: 9-12 lasagne sheets (depending on the size of your baking dish)
method: In a large pan, heat the olive oil over a low heat. Fry chopped garlic until softened.
Add the mince and fry on a medium heat until golden.
Stir in chopped tomatoes and stock. Simmer for 15 mins, or until the liquid has reduced.
Meanwhile, make the white sauce. Melt the butter in a small saucepan over a low heat and add the flour. Whisk until combined and cook on low for 1-2 mins.
Remove from the heat and gradually whisk in the milk until you have a loose sauce. Season. Return to a gentle heat and whisk constantly until the sauce thickens.
Preheat the oven to gas 6, 200°C, fan 180°C.
Layer up the lasagne in a baking dish, starting with a third each of the meat, then the pasta, then the white sauce. Repeat twice.
Bake in the oven for 40-45 mins, until piping hot and crisp and bubbling on top. Serve immediately.
notes: Budget swap: Use frozen beef and pork mince mix instead of fresh mince.
Freezing: Once cooled, freeze in an airtight container for 1-3 months. Defrost in fridge overnight before reheating until piping hot.
